properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 5:04PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 503 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along
a line extending from 17 miles southwest of Fowler to 18 miles east
of Walsenburg. Movement was east at 20 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph and pea size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Delhi, and Thatcher.
properties.areaDesc: Pueblo Vicinity/Pueblo County Below 6300 Feet; Walsenburg Vicinity/Upper Huerfano River Basin Below 7500 Ft; Trinidad Vicinity/Western Las Animas County Below 7500 Ft; La Junta Vicinity/Otero County; Eastern Las Animas County

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 5:00PM MDT until July 23 at 6:15PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 500 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain have moved across the Middle and South Aspen Acres Burn Scar. Up
to 0.7 inches of rain have fallen in the Muddy Creek Basin with up to
a half inch falling across a larger portion of the southern Aspen
Acres Burn scars. Flooding impacts will continue until the water has
a chance to recede. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly. Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris
flow. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other
loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms producing
flash flooding in and around the Middle and South Aspen
Acres Burn Scar.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around the
Middle and South Aspen Acres Burn Scar.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Rye, San Isabel, Beulah and Colorado City.

This includes the following high risk locations...
Beulah Water Intake.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Saint Charles River, Muddy Creek and its tributaries, Spring Branch
and South Creek and its tributaries.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 4:44PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 444 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 6
miles north of Jackson Reservoir, or 21 miles northwest of Fort
Morgan, moving southeast at 25 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and pea size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor damage to outdoor objects is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Fort Morgan, Brush, Wiggins, Log Lane Village, Hillrose, Weldona,
Antelope Springs, Goodrich, Jackson Reservoir, Orchard, Snyder,
Fort Morgan Airport and Union.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 4:32PM MDT until July 23 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
FFWBOU

The National Weather Service in Denver has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Southeastern Elbert County in east central Colorado...
Central Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* Until 730 PM MDT.

* At 432 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1 and 2.5 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 1 inch are possible
in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, gulches,
roads, and poor drainage and low-lying areas.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of Southeastern Elbert and Central Lincoln
Counties.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Apache Creek, Mustang Creek, Antelope Creek, South Rush Creek,
Middle Rush Creek, Long Creek, Rush Creek and North Rush Creek.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 23 at 3:52PM MDT until July 23 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire Burn Scar
is possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire Burn Scar in Ouray County Colorado,
including Dexter and Cutler Creek drainages and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 700 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 346 PM MDT, the probability of heavy rainfall will
increase through at least 7 pm MDT. If thunderstorms develop
over the Gold Mountain burn scar, debris flows are likely
within Cutler and Dexter Creek drainages, including Highway
550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
